rag_rules: Fix AGREE syntax errors by correcting invalid input tokens like '[' or '->' in contract expressions.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must preserve type consistency and avoid package-qualified references not present in the model.
Repair AGREE diagnostics by aligning helper declarations with expression types and using only defined or imported identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax for assume, guarantee, eq, and assign.
Component type contracts reference only visible features; implementation contracts may include subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model during repair.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses using visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics related to syntax errors in unrelated components
Model fusion or architecture grounding rules
